headline: On Algeria, Macron has a goal for his last year in office: Reconciliation
description: The French president, who began his first term aiming to ease historical tensions, now faces the most severe crisis in bilateral relations since Algeria's independence in 1962.

headline: Trump halts Hormuz escort operation after one day
Kyiv's unilateral ceasefire already broken by Russian strikes
'With the withdrawal of US troops from Germany, Berlin is facing a historic dilemma'
description: Donald Trump's announcement of the withdrawal of US troops is forcing Berlin to confront the long-standing taboo on its own defense, explains Paul Maurice, a specialist in Franco-German relations.

headline: Sweden's conservatives are now normalizing the far-right
description: With legislative elections scheduled for September 13 approaching, the Conservatives, Liberals and Christian Democrats have signaled their willingness to govern with the Sweden Democrats, the far-right party that enabled them to return to power in 2022.

headline: Chinese artist Gao Zhen and his family stuck in repressive legal limbo over his Mao sculptures
description: Authorities are accusing the visual artist, a US resident, over works created in the 2000s that explicitly criticized Chairman Mao's regime. His wife holds out faint hope that Donald Trump's upcoming visit to Beijing, on May 14 and 15, can help her husband's case.

headline: German Chancellor Merz hits a new low one year after taking office
description: At the helm of a coalition plagued by disagreements and criticized for his verbal gaffes, the German head of government is the most unpopular chancellor since the founding of the Federal Republic.

Louvre President Christophe Leribault: 'I am not here to undo the work of my predecessors'
description: In an interview, the successor to Laurence des Cars as head of the museum explains how he intends to make his mark with humility, courage and ambition.

headline: Jean Pisani-Ferry: 'The United States has lost its moral authority for good'
description: The United States is turning its back on democracy and freedom – values the nation once embodied – and is now replacing them with the law of the strongest and a culture of deal-making. Donald Trump has caused irreparable harm to the world's leading power, argues the economist in his column.

headline: Saint Pierre and Miquelon, a pioneering example of climate change adaptation
description: This year, residents of a town in the French overseas territory off the coast of Canada are set to move into a new village. This relocation, France's first planned effort to move an entire town, is the result of a lengthy process.
